source_pr.title: planner: make sure limit outputs no more columns than its child
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/25344

Problem Summary:

panic may happen when limit is on top of window functions

### What is changed and how it works?

What's Changed:

Prune columns for child operator first, and then re-assign schema for limit.

How it Works:

Make sure limit outputs no more columns than its child


### Related changes

N/A

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- Unit test


Side effects

N/A

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- Make sure limit outputs no more columns than its child

symptoms: tk.MustQuery("select count(a) f1, row_number() over (order by count(a)) as f2 from t limit 1").Check(testkit.Rows("1 1")) (planner/core/integration_test.go)
failure_signature: tk.MustQuery("select count(a) f1, row_number() over (order by count(a)) as f2 from t limit 1").Check(testkit.Rows("1 1")) (planner/core/integration_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it asserted an exact plan/EXPLAIN output that can vary between runs. Exact plan/EXPLAIN assertions are brittle when optimizer choices can vary. This PR stabilizes it by: tk.MustQuery("select count(a) f1, row_number() over (order by count(a)) as f2 from t limit 1").Check(testkit.Rows("1 1")) (planner/core/integration_test.go).
fix_pattern: tk.MustQuery("select count(a) f1, row_number() over (order by count(a)) as f2 from t limit 1").Check(testkit.Rows("1 1")) (planner/core/integration_test.go)

evidence: evidence: tk.MustQuery("select count(a) f1, row_number() over (order by count(a)) as f2 from t limit 1").Check(testkit.Rows("1 1")) (planner/core/integration_test.go)
